join_order.probe: orders(sequential scan with orderdate zone pruning)
join_order.notes: Resolve the runtime BUILDING code, build a dense customer-membership bitset keyed by c_custkey, then scan only o_custkey and o_orderdate sequentially to mark qualifying order rows in ascending order-row order.

join_order.build: qualifying_order_rows(bitset keyed by orders row id)
join_order.probe: lineitem_orderkey_groups(sequential row-aligned scan with shipdate block states)
join_order.notes: Scan lineitem_orderkey_groups in order-row order; for set bits, use lineitem_shipdate_zone_map on the run's containing block to skip zero-result runs, fast-accept whole runs without l_shipdate loads, and fall back to row-level checks only for mixed blocks. Fetch o_orderkey, o_orderdate, and o_shippriority only for positive-revenue rows.

data_structures.dim_filter: bitset(1500001) customer membership plus bitset(15000000) qualifying order rows
data_structures.fact_join: direct_array(row-aligned lineitem_orderkey_groups with 600-entry lineitem shipdate block-state array)
data_structures.aggregation: array_direct(per-order scalar revenue accumulation with sparse late materialization and thread-local topk heaps)

indexes.purpose: enumerate BUILDING customer rows and populate the dense customer-membership bitset
skip and fast-accept order blocks while building the qualifying-order bitset from a sequential orders scan
scan row-aligned contiguous lineitem runs in order-row order without hashing or re-scanning l_orderkey
classify each 100000-row lineitem block as skip, accept, or fallback so shipdate checks and payload loads are only done when necessary

optimization_notes: Root cause: the best current plan still does lineitem work that is not proportional to output, because every BUILDING-plus-orderdate qualifying order descends into its lineitem run and performs row-level shipdate checks even when lineitem_shipdate_zone_map can prove the whole surrounding block rejects or accepts, and it eagerly streams order payload columns that only surviving rows need. Strategy category C (work elimination): keep the successful cache-resident customer bitset and sequential orders filtering path, materialize a compact qualifying-order bitset, then scan row-aligned lineitem_orderkey_groups sequentially while using lineitem_shipdate_zone_map to skip zero-result runs, fast-accept whole runs without l_shipdate loads, and late-materialize o_orderkey, o_orderdate, and o_shippriority only for positive-revenue rows. This removes unnecessary per-row lineitem checks and unnecessary order payload reads while preserving sequential access on the large structures.
